headline: Micron’s COMPUTEX AI Memory Launch Meets Lofty Valuation Expectations
summary: Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU) revealed a broad lineup of AI focused memory and storage products at COMPUTEX 2026. The company highlighted HBM4 36GB modules, 256GB SOCAMM2 modules, high speed DDR5 RDIMMs, and high density data center SSDs. Micron also outlined how these products target AI workloads across data centers, PCs, vehicles, and embedded systems. Micron sits at the center of the memory and storage market, where AI training and inference place heavy demands on bandwidth, capacity,...

headline: Marvell among the gainers in latest AI trade upswing
summary: Marvell Technology (MRVL) stock has rocketed higher by over 25% after Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang declared the custom chipmaker to be “the next trillion-dollar company" at Computex Taipei 2026. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Ines Ferré and Brooke DiPalma and EMJ Capital founder and president Eric Jackson examine the latest headlines moving stocks in the AI trade landscape. This news comes as Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares surge by over 30% in response to the tech company beat past fiscal second quarter estimates on adjusted earnings and revenue.

content.title: Marvell among the gainers in latest AI trade upswing
content.description:
Marvell Technology (MRVL) stock has rocketed higher by over 25% after Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang declared the custom chipmaker to be “the next trillion-dollar company" at Computex Taipei 2026.

Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Ines Ferré and Brooke DiPalma and EMJ Capital founder and president Eric Jackson examine the latest headlines moving stocks in the AI trade landscape.

This news comes as Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares surge by over 30% in response to the tech company beat past fiscal second quarter estimates on adjusted earnings and revenue.

This news comes as Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) shares surge by over 30% in response to the tech company beat past fiscal second quarter estimates on adjusted earnings and revenue.

headline: AI demand, earnings optimism lift tech giants' market value in May
summary: The world's most valuable technology companies with the exception of Alphabet added billions ‌of dollars in market value in May, ‌as upbeat earnings outlooks and strong demand for AI chips fuelled ​a rally in their shares. Apple, memory chipmakers Micron Technology, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix led the gains, adding $598 billion, $512 billion, $481 billion and $377 billion, respectively, in market value. Their ‌valuations rose to $4.58 ⁠trillion, $1.09 trillion, $1.10 trillion and $1.34 trillion, respectively.

content.title: AI demand, earnings optimism lift tech giants' market value in May
content.summary: The world's most valuable technology companies with the exception of Alphabet added billions ‌of dollars in market value in May, ‌as upbeat earnings outlooks and strong demand for AI chips fuelled ​a rally in their shares. Apple, memory chipmakers Micron Technology, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix led the gains, adding $598 billion, $512 billion, $481 billion and $377 billion, respectively, in market value. Their ‌valuations rose to $4.58 ⁠trillion, $1.09 trillion, $1.10 trillion and $1.34 trillion, respectively.

headline: Applied Materials: The AI Stock That Wins No Matter Which Chip Wins
summary: When investors think about AI winners, the usual names come to mind: Nvidia (NVDA), TSMC, or memory makers like Micron (MU). Semiconductor equipment giant Applied Materials (AMAT) rarely gets the same attention, despite supplying the tools needed to manufacture nearly every critical component of an AI chip.

headline: Analysis-How a nudge from Nvidia propelled frugal Micron into the AI boom and a $1 trillion market cap
summary: Micron Technology's march toward a $1 trillion valuation is nothing if not dramatic: a year ago it was a little over $100 billion.  That surge, though, was not built on its famed frugality, but on a nearly too-late push from Nvidia that ‌pulled the U.S. memory chipmaker into the center of the AI boom.  Three years ago, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met Micron boss Sanjay Mehrotra and outlined how he expected the memory market to evolve, Huang said in a media interview last month.

headline: Wall Street hangs around its records as the AI boom keeps growing
summary: The U.S. stock market is ticking toward more records Tuesday as winners of the artificial-intelligence boom keep driving higher.  Marvell Technology leaped 29.5% toward its best day in three years after Nvidia’s CEO, Jensen Huang, suggested at a conference in Taiwan that Marvell could be “the next trillion-dollar company.”  Hewlett Packard Enterprise's stock soared 16.5% after it reported a profit for the latest quarter that blew past analysts’ expectations.

content.title: Wall Street hangs around its records as the AI boom keeps growing
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is ticking toward more records Tuesday as winners of the artificial-intelligence boom keep driving higher.  Marvell Technology leaped 29.5% toward its best day in three years after Nvidia’s CEO, Jensen Huang, suggested at a conference in Taiwan that Marvell could be “the next trillion-dollar company.”  Hewlett Packard Enterprise's stock soared 16.5% after it reported a profit for the latest quarter that blew past analysts’ expectations.

thumbnail.caption: Traders Edward McCarthy, left, and Robert Charmak work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Monday, June 1, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)
